_role: Reference data dictionary — NOT loaded by 03_normalize.py. Normalization logic is hardcoded because the conditional behavior (R-file priority, S-suffix mapping, NTAR exclusion) is too complex for flat config. This JSON documents field semantics and known issues for human and agent reference.

field_mappings._note: Legacy column name -> modern column name. Apply after .upper().strip() on all column names.

identical_fields._note: Fields that have the same name in legacy and modern. Listed for completeness.

dropped_fields._note: Legacy fields with no modern equivalent. Safe to discard during normalization.

COUNT.description: Number of trade transactions aggregated into the row

DISTGROUP.impact: None - derivable from DEPE port codes

NTAR.description: National Transportation Analysis Region — 89 multicounty regions based on exporter ZIP code
NTAR.found_in: D5B, D6B (1994-2002 export tables, 'State of Exporter' variant)
NTAR.impact: Low - not used in standard analysis
NTAR.handling: D5B/D6B are EXCLUDED from normalization entirely (not just the field). They use NTAR instead of state codes, making them incompatible with DOT1's state×port structure. D5A/D6A already cover the same exports with proper state geography.

modern_table_schemas._note: The 3 modern (2007+) table column definitions. Target schema for normalization.

DOT3.description: Port x Commodity (no legacy equivalent, 2007+ only)

legacy_table_definitions._note: Maps legacy table numbers to their dimensions and modern equivalents. Trade direction is encoded in the TABLE NUMBER: D03-D06 = Exports, D09-D12 = Imports. ALL D-tables are SURFACE-ONLY (DISAGMOT 4-9). Air/vessel modes (1, 3) never appear in D-tables. Source: BTS README_9.TXT (1993), README4.TXT (1994).

exports._note: D03-D06 are SURFACE EXPORT tables. They use ORSTATE/EXSTATE (origin/exporter) and SCH_B (Schedule B export commodity). Missing CONTCODE, SHIPWT — these only appear in import tables.

D03.description: Exports to Mexico — Commodity x Mexican State

D04.description: Exports to Canada — Commodity x Canadian Province

D05.description: Exports to Mexico — State x Port

D06.description: Exports to Canada — State x Port

imports._note: D09-D12 are SURFACE IMPORT tables. They use DESTATE (destination state) and TSUSA (import commodity). They carry additional fields (CONTCODE, CHARGES, SHIPWT) not present in export tables. DISAGMOT values: 4 (mail), 5 (truck), 6 (rail), 7 (pipeline), 8 (other), 9 (FTZ). No air (1) or vessel (3).

D09.description: Imports from Mexico — Commodity x State (no province)

D10.description: Imports from Canada — Commodity x State x Province

D11.description: Imports from Mexico — State x Port (no province)

D12.description: Imports from Canada — State x Port x Province

air_vessel._note: AV1-AV12 are AIR/VESSEL tables. Only exist Nov 2003 - Dec 2006. DISAGMOT values: 1 (air), 3 (vessel) only. 12 tables mirror D-table structure. No STATMOYR — month/year parsed from filename. Naming: 2003: av{table}.dbf (Nov), av{table}12.dbf (Dec). 2004-2006: av{table}{MM}{YY}.dbf.

AV1.description: Air/Vessel Exports to Mexico — Commodity x State

AV2.description: Air/Vessel Exports to Canada — Commodity x State

AV3.description: Air/Vessel Exports to Mexico — State x Port

AV4.description: Air/Vessel Exports to Mexico — Commodity x Port

AV5.description: Air/Vessel Exports to Canada — State x Port

AV6.description: Air/Vessel Exports to Canada — Commodity x Port

AV7.description: Air/Vessel Imports from Mexico — Commodity x State

AV8.description: Air/Vessel Imports from Canada — Commodity x State

AV9.description: Air/Vessel Imports from Mexico — State x Port

AV10.description: Air/Vessel Imports from Mexico — Commodity x Port

AV11.description: Air/Vessel Imports from Canada — State x Port

AV12.description: Air/Vessel Imports from Canada — Commodity x Port

ab_suffix_rules._note: From Apr 1994 to 2002, export tables (D03-D06) were split into A/B variants. A and B are BOTH exports — the suffix encodes GEOGRAPHIC METHODOLOGY, not trade direction. B-tables were dropped in 2003. Source: README4.TXT, README5.TXT.
ab_suffix_rules.A: State of Origin — where goods entered the foreign trade pipeline
ab_suffix_rules.B: State/NTAR of Exporter — where the exporting company is located (D3B/D4B use EXSTATE; D5B/D6B use NTAR)
ab_suffix_rules.S: State of Origin — used in 1993 and early 1994 before the 'A' suffix was introduced (e.g., D5APR93S.DBF). Maps to the 'A' variant.
ab_suffix_rules.bts_rationale: Census reports that neither measure provides a true representation of the production origin of exports, because the state of origin may be the state that contains a consolidation point.
ab_suffix_rules.important: Trade direction (export vs import) and country are encoded in the TABLE NUMBER, not in filename suffixes. D03-D06 = exports, D09-D12 = imports. Suffixes D, J, M, N, O do NOT exist in actual BTS filenames.
ab_suffix_rules.d5b_d6b_exclusion: D5B/D6B use NTAR (89 multicounty regions) instead of state codes. They are EXCLUDED from normalization — incompatible with DOT1 state×port structure. D5A/D6A cover the same exports with proper state geography.

date_mappings._note: STATMOYR changed format mid-stream. Modern files use separate MONTH and YEAR columns.

legacy_table_evolution._note: Which table types exist in which time periods. Trade direction is always encoded in the table number: D03-D06 = exports, D09-D12 = imports.

1993_apr_dec.description: Original format. No A/B split, but 'S' suffix used for State of Origin.
1993_apr_dec.notes: 1993 uses SCH_B_GRP and TSUSA_GRP instead of SCH_B/TSUSA. Also has MEXREGION, USREGION, DISTGROUP columns. D5...S files exist alongside D05 files; S denotes State of Origin and maps to D5A.

1994_transition.description: Jan-Mar: old format (including 'S' suffix for State of Origin). Apr-Dec: export tables split into A/B variants (State of Origin vs State of Exporter).
1994_transition.notes: A = State of Origin, B = State/NTAR of Exporter. Both A and B are EXPORTS. Import tables (D09-D12) are unchanged. Pre-April 'S' files map to 'A'.

1995_to_2002.description: Stable era. Export tables have A/B split; import tables unchanged.
1995_to_2002.notes: 1995 has 26 revision files (X-prefix and R-prefix) for Jan-Mar and Jul. Use revisions instead of originals.

2003_to_2006.description: B-variants (State of Exporter) dropped. A-tables (State of Origin) continue as EXPORT-ONLY. Imports remain in D09-D12.
2003_to_2006.notes: A-tables still contain only exports (confirmed by ORSTATE and SCH_B columns). They do NOT contain imports.

2007_onward.notes: All countries, directions, and modes in single files. TRDTYPE and COUNTRY are explicit columns.

case_sensitivity.description: 2006 DBF files use lowercase column names (e.g., 'disagmot', 'depe'). All other years use uppercase.
case_sensitivity.fix: Apply .upper().strip() to all column names before mapping.

trailing_spaces.description: 2006 DBF files have trailing spaces in column names (e.g., 'disagmot  ').
trailing_spaces.fix: Apply .strip() to all column names before mapping.

revision_files_1995.description: 26 revision files with X-prefix and R-prefix exist for Jan-Mar 1995 and Jul 1995.
revision_files_1995.fix: Use revision files INSTEAD OF original files for those months.

may_1993_date_mismatch.description: 4 files in May 1993 (D04MAY93, D06MAY93, D10MAY93, D12MAY93) contain STATMOYR=0493 (April), not 0593.
may_1993_date_mismatch.fix: These are likely re-released April data with May filenames. Trust STATMOYR value over filename.

commodity_code_1993.description: 1993 uses SCH_B_GRP and TSUSA_GRP which may be grouped/aggregated commodity codes rather than individual HS 2-digit codes.
commodity_code_1993.fix: Verify that values match the same coding scheme used from 1994+. Flag if discrepancies found.

b_tables_dropped_2003.description: B-variants (State of Exporter methodology) were dropped in 2003. A-tables continue as export-only. Imports remain in D09-D12.
b_tables_dropped_2003.fix: No special handling needed — A-tables are still exports, D09-D12 are still imports. Trade direction comes from the table number.

av_files_2003_2006.description: Air/vessel (AV) tables exist only Nov 2003 - Dec 2006. Before Nov 2003, TransBorder was surface-only. 12 AV tables parallel the D-table structure. AV4/6/10/12 provide Port×Commodity (DOT3-equivalent) for air/vessel modes only.
av_files_2003_2006.fix: Parse AV filenames separately (different naming convention from D-tables). Set MONTH/YEAR from filename since AV files lack STATMOYR.

d_tables_surface_only.description: All D-prefix tables (D03-D12, including A/B variants) contain ONLY surface modes (DISAGMOT 4-9). Air (1) and Vessel (3) never appear. This was verified against actual DBF files across all years 1993-2006.
d_tables_surface_only.fix: Document clearly that pre-Nov-2003 data is surface-only. Air/vessel totals for 1993-Oct 2003 are not available in TransBorder.

MEXSTATE_BN.description: Baja California Norte code BN used erroneously Apr 1994 - May 1998

fields_missing_in_legacy._note: Modern columns that have no legacy equivalent. These will be null/empty for pre-2007 records.

CONTCODE_exports.description: Containerization code not available in legacy export tables (D03-D06). Present in import tables (D09-D12).

SHIPWT_exports.description: Shipping weight not available in legacy export tables (D03-D06). Present in import tables (D09-D12).

FREIGHT_CHARGES_mexico_exports.description: Freight charges not in legacy Mexico export tables (D03, D05). Available in Canada export tables (D04, D06) as FREIGHT, and in all import tables (D09-D12) as CHARGES.

DOT3_pre_2007.description: Surface Port x Commodity cross-tabulation did not exist before January 2007. However, air/vessel Port x Commodity data is available from AV tables (AV4/6/10/12) for Nov 2003 - Dec 2006.
DOT3_pre_2007.available_from: Nov 2003 (air/vessel only via AV tables), Jan 2007 (all modes via DOT3)
